# CANYON — Probing Semantic Grounding in LLMs
> A multilingual white-box + black-box framework testing Geoffrey Hinton's
> grounding hypothesis against the stochastic-parrot hypothesis in LLMs.
> Published at https://pedjaurosevic.github.io/canyon/ by Predrag Urosevic.
> Repo: https://github.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon

## About

CANYON tests whether LLMs build internal world models (Hinton's view) or merely
stitch together likely word sequences (the stochastic-parrot view). It asks
adversarial trick questions across six languages (en, zh, ja, ru, de, es)
where the statistically obvious answer is wrong, then checks whether the model
overrides the trap. Results are expressed as a Stochastic Parrot Index (SPI):

    SPI = 0.4·CP + 0.4·CR + 0.2·SI

- **CP** (Counterfactual Plasticity): Holding an altered law of physics across turns
- **CR** (Contextual Realignment): Resolving ambiguous wording toward the sensible referent
- **SI** (Semantic Invariance): Recognising an abstract contradiction or joke

## Key files

- README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/main/README.md
- Whitepaper: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/main/WHITEPAPER.md
- Full dataset: https://github.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/tree/main/dataset
- Results directory: https://github.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/tree/main/results
- Interactive leaderboard: https://pedjaurosevic.github.io/canyon/
- All test suites: https://github.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/tree/main/canyon/suites
- Metric engine source: https://github.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/blob/main/canyon/metrics.py
- Build scripts: https://github.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/tree/main/scripts

## Core dataset

The dataset contains 35 prompts across 7 languages (including Serbian reference),
525 scored conversation turns, and 18 full-transcript model runs.

- Prompts file (JSONL, full schema): https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/main/dataset/canyon_prompts.jsonl
- Conversations file (JSONL, full transcripts): https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/main/dataset/canyon_conversations.jsonl
- Dataset README with schema: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pedjaurosevic/canyon/main/dataset/README.md

## Models tested

- deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash (chat API)
- deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro (chat API)
- gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.5 (via Codex agent)
- claude-haiku-4.5, claude-sonnet-4.6, claude-opus-4.8 (via Claude Code agent)
- novita/qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct (chat API)
- novita/qwen/qwen3.5-122b-a10b (chat API)
- novita/meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct (chat API)
- novita/meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct (chat API)
- novita/meta-llama/llama-4-maverick-17b-128e-instruct-fp8 (chat API)
- novita/google/gemma-4-31b-it (chat API)
- novita/google/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it (chat API)
- openai/gemma-4-12B-it-Q4_K_M.gguf (local black-box over llama.cpp)
- Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct (local white-box with activations)

## Key finding

Today's strongest models behave much more like grounded world-models than like
pure stochastic parrots on these adversarial-by-design questions: they override
the statistically obvious-but-wrong answer, they hold a counterfactual world
across turns, and their deep layers reorganise onto the situation rather than
the surface words. However, this is a *small* experiment — differences below
~0.05 SPI are within run-to-run noise.
